mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict returns beyond a 1-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply weakness, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not systematically decline when both series contract simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks rather than healthy growth, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO rises during high inflation periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation falls below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects robust consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically leads positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window, or housing starts rise while SPX forward returns are consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it signals a sharp deceleration in aggregate demand that typically precedes equity drawdowns, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 100,000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >50% of historical instances, or SPX recovers within 30 days of the trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and unemployment rate indicates a policy-induced demand shock that historically triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with labor weakness
failure_condition: Forward returns do not systematically decline when both series rise simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, rising consumer credit amplifies leverage-driven consumption, which historically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 exceeds 3.0% and RSAFS rises, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during low-growth periods (<1.5%).
